mnemonics: Noise Busters: CPD (Clean Picture Desired) - Use Convolution to Process Degradations. Imagine a team of 'Noise Buster' ghosts using convolution operations to clean a degraded image.
keyPoints: The goal is to recover an original image from a degraded (noisy/blurry) version using a mathematical model of the degradation.
This often involves inverse filtering or iterative algorithms to estimate and remove noise and blur.
Reconstruction often uses frequency domain techniques (like the Fourier Transform) to analyze and manipulate image data.
commonMistakes: Assuming all noise is additive and Gaussian; different types (e.g., salt-and-pepper) require different filters (median vs. Wiener).
Attempting inverse filtering without considering noise, which amplifies noise when the degradation filter's values are very small or zero.
